import collections
import heapq

def top_k_frequent(nums: list[int], k: int) -> list[int]:
    if k == len(nums):
        return list(set(nums)) # Optimization for edge case if all elements are frequent

    # 1. Count the frequency of each number
    freq_map = collections.Counter(nums)

    # 2. Use a min-heap to keep track of the k most frequent elements
    # The heap will store tuples of (frequency, number)
    min_heap = []

    for num, freq in freq_map.items():
        # Push the (frequency, number) tuple onto the heap
        heapq.heappush(min_heap, (freq, num))
        # If the heap size exceeds k, remove the element with the smallest frequency
        if len(min_heap) > k:
            heapq.heappop(min_heap)

    # 3. Extract the elements from the heap
    # The heap now contains the k most frequent elements
    result = [item[1] for item in min_heap]

    return result
